title: piscidia longifolia
intro: Piscidia longifolia, also known as Jamaica dogwood, is a plant species native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Jamaica dogwood include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as a reduction in inflammation and pain. Jamaica dogwood can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, with the latter often being used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
